target.name: Congressional Artificial Intelligence Caucus
target.notes_excerpt: Bipartisan House caucus promoting AI education and policy dialogue. Originally co-chaired by Reps. Anna Eshoo (D-CA) and Michael McCaul (R-TX); Don Beyer (D-VA) was an active co-chair figure. Convenes members to engage on AI legislation, coordinates with the House AI Task Force, and maintains a nonpartisan pro-innovation stance.

target.name: House Bipartisan Task Force on Artificial Intelligence
target.notes_excerpt: Bipartisan U.S. House task force created in Feb 2024 by Speaker Johnson and Leader Jeffries, co-chaired by Reps. Obernolte and Lieu. Released a 273-page final report in Dec 2024 with 66 findings and 89 recommendations across 15 policy areas. Sunset at end of 118th Congress; no successor body established in the 119th Congress as of early 2025.

source.name: Anna Eshoo
source.notes_excerpt: Anna Eshoo is a U.S. Representative who served California's 16th Congressional District from 1993 to 2025, retiring in January 2025. She co-chairs the Congressional Artificial Intelligence (AI) Caucus and was named one of Time magazine's 100 most influential people in AI in 2023. She served as Chair of the Health Subcommittee and was the first woman in the history of Congress to hold that position

target.name: Sam Altman
target.notes_excerpt: Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI, the artificial intelligence research organization that developed ChatGPT. He became CEO of OpenAI in 2019 after co-founding the organization in 2015. In November 2023, he was briefly fired by OpenAI's board of directors but was reinstated five days later. His net worth was estimated at US$3.3 billion by Forbes in March 2026.
